title: Pinalia sinica
taxonomy: Pinalia sinica is classified in the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the monocot group of flowering plants. The genus Pinalia is also part of the subtribe Coelogyneinae. Orchidaceae is the third-largest family of flowering plants, comprising over 30,000 species. Pinalia sinica's classification highlights its evolutionary connections within the orchid family.

intro: Pinalia sinica, also known as the "Chinese Pinalia" or "Yinyanghuo," is a type of orchid plant native to the tropical regions of China and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve cardiovascular health, and prevent certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Pinalia sinica include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Pinalia sinica is commonly used to make various herbal preparations, such as capsules, teas, and tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation, boost energy, and support overall well-being.
